_readme: RULES-2 BUILD assumption block PROPOSED-ASM-1440..1459 — EMITTED by the build role for central registration by the coordinator.

This build has NO write access to registry/assumptions.jsonl and did not touch it (same discipline as the design doc's 1420..1439 block, which remains the design-level record; nothing here amends a frozen object).
Owner: fable-build-1, 2026-07-12. All tags provisional; no feasibility conclusion is stated or implied.

CORRECTED 2026-07-12 (rework-1, cross-vendor prereg review): 1440 (per-arm/rung efficiency ledger), 1446 (byte-identical 24-option surface), 1448 (LABEL-derangement replaces item-Sattolo), 1452 (padding asymmetry REMOVED; common 23-option gap surface), 1454 (resolution_path added; re-planned costs), 1458 (registered SESOI 0.05 decision band; REAL Holm step-down with FT-seed-aware bootstrap p-values).

CORRECTED AGAIN 2026-07-12 (rework-2, fable-build-2 — cross-vendor review items 5/6/9 + the rules-1-b supersede): 1440 (merge_shards.py/validate_mock.py added; sharded parallel launch), 1442 (TOKEN-level name-collision guard; wv0-elvis-presley excluded+listed; corpus regenerated + repinned 3465e63d...), 1443 (exclusion ledger lists component ids), 1445 (c8 rework-2 regeneration sha cb3fecd3...; gate PASS 0/858 unchanged), 1446 (refusal-after-infill-cue grammar disclosure), 1447 (|B2| train = 11,411), 1448 (rel23 10,121 / mw2 1,290 / 18 flips; 0/11,411), 1450 (B4 imports the RULES-1-B freeze bytes 1f423ac0... after the 2026-07-12 VOID of rules-1's GPU run), 1452 (every rules-2 surface rebuilt on the rules-1-b direction-explicit frame contract; typing cue added), 1454 (re-planned sharded costs; per-job 12 h bound), 1455 (green mock PINNED as results/mock-validation.json incl. shard/merge parity + verdict-mapping), 1459 (hash-before-import G1 ordering per review item 6; shard-merge protocol; PINNED-AT-INPUTS sha 56c713d4...).

rows.claim: The RULES-2 harness is poc/rules-2/{materialise_closure.py, rules2_runner.py, merge_shards.py, validate_mock.py, inputs/rules2-manifest.json, modal/modal_rules2.py} + analysis/rules_2.py, implementing the design's arms as B0/B1/B2/B3/B4/B5/c1p plus the CPU artifacts c8 (train-bytes projection gate) and c4 (trivial floors, descriptive); poc/rules-1, poc/f2b-transfer, poc/f2b, poc/deconf-b and poc/modal trees are NOT modified — rules-1 drivers and the f2bt scorer are imported byte-identical under sha pins. EFFICIENCY LEDGER (rework-1, review fix 4): the runner records per-cell (arm/rung/seed) wall seconds and peak memory (CUDA allocator peak reset per cell on GPU; process peak RSS with the instrument NAMED per entry on CPU) in results.eval_ledger/training_ledger, and rows carry tokens_in/flops_formula/attempts/engine_us, so the pinned analysis emits PER-ARM/RUNG peak memory, GPU-h, USD, tokens, FLOPs, the identical-S-out per-query cost, B4 attempts + engine CPU, and the break-even N* on the common 23-option surface. REWORK-2: the campaign launches as INDEPENDENT parallel Modal jobs (one per FT arm x seed x rung, per B4 seed, per eval-only arm; 17 R1-tier + 7 R2-tier), splittable across Modal accounts; merge_shards.py reconstructs the canonical results pair under fail-closed cross-shard pin/surface/coverage assertions, with mock-proven analysis parity vs the monolithic path (identical on every field except the disclosed process-measured metrics).
rows.backing_ref: docs/next/design/rules-2-train-time.md SS2.3/2.5; poc/rules-2/inputs/rules2-manifest.json pins; analysis/rules_2.py build_efficiency_ledger
rows.rationale: One new module tree; every reused byte pin-verified; the frozen estate untouched; the PROPOSED-ASM-1429 price table is measured per arm and rung, not aggregated.

rows.claim: Training-corpus construction rule: one example per (component, cell); context = ALL stated facts of the component (kinship inventory) verbalised in the RULES-1 canonical rendering + the UNA line; families = {1 stated, 2 entailed {chain E3, cover E1 via parent-abstraction, typing E2 via class-abstraction}, 3 refusal E5 (first 4 sha-ordered insufficient-premise pairs, engine refusal asserted per cell)}; every family-2 example carries why() proof text, regime tag {owl-rl, policy} and proof depth in sidecar fields that NEVER enter B2 training text.
rows.rationale: The treatment content is exactly the pinned engine's output over prompt-shaped contexts — the same temporary-world regime S-out evaluates.

rows.claim: The synthetic expansion replicates the nsk1-eval family SHAPE (3 generations, 8 people, 8 gendered-parent edges) with FRESH seed 20260712 and FRESH invented name pools; surface-name disjointness from BOTH eval corpora (nsk1-clutrr item lexicons, nsk1-eval lexicon) is asserted fail-closed (ERR_NAME_COLLISION) at TOKEN level — the full name OR ANY whitespace token of a training surface name matching the token-expanded eval-name set rejects it (REWORK-2, review item 5: the former FULL-NAME-only test admitted world-v0 text carrying the CLUTRR given-name tokens 'Gladys' and 'Lisa' inside 'Gladys Presley'/'Lisa Marie Presley'); world-v0 components with a token-level collision are excluded, COUNTED and LISTED by component id (1 in the real build: wv0-elvis-presley); the corpus was regenerated and repinned (kot-corpus-hash 3465e63d...).
rows.backing_ref: PROPOSED-ASM-1421; poc/nsk1/gen_nsk1_corpus.py (shape); data/rules2-train/manifest.json name_disjointness + exclusion_ledger; cross-vendor prereg review 2026-07-12 item 5
rows.rationale: Zero string overlap between training names and the primary eval stratum is the memorisation split's mechanical premise; asserted, not assumed.

rows.claim: world-v0 enters training filtered to the RULES-1 closed inventory (mother/father relations, man/woman classes); out-of-inventory records (72 relations, 45 classes) and the 3 components conflict-closed by world-v0's planted violations are EXCLUDED WITH COUNTS in data/rules2-train/manifest.json exclusion_ledger — never silently; REWORK-2 adds the token-level name-collision exclusion (count AND component-id list) to the same ledger.
rows.backing_ref: data/world-v0/manifest.json plantedViolations; twin_engine conflict detection (CAX-DW, PRP-FP+UNA)
rows.rationale: The engine fail-closes on conflicted components by design; a disclosed deterministic exclusion preserves both the fail-closed discipline and corpus reproducibility.

rows.claim: Splits: dev = the last 20 synthetic families (loss-sanity ledger only, never selection); S-held = family-2 cells with sha256('sheld|'+id) fraction < 0.10 among non-dev cells (pinned, 1,249 cells in the rework-2 real build). DISCLOSURE: cover (E1) cells share their (pair, answer) string with trained family-1 stated cells of the same component and ARE flat-lookup-recoverable (c8 S-held by_kind: cover 142/142, chain 0/125, typing 0/33); S-held is descriptive-only and read stratified by kind — the S-out claim is untouched.
rows.rationale: The one string-leak the split construction creates is measured and disclosed by the instrument itself rather than argued away.

rows.claim: c8 operationalisation: GS-family FLAT lookup over the pinned B2 TRAINING bytes — a QA index (trained question pair -> answer) plus an aligned fact-line index ((subject-name, object-name) -> stated word), NO joins, NO inference; gate G2 = S-out recovered accuracy <= 0.10 (chance-class ceiling; measured 0/858 = 0.0 in the real build; rework-2 regeneration from the token-guard materialiser bytes, artifact sha cb3fecd3..., gate numbers unchanged), run pre-GPU together with the pin gate; failure => INSTRUMENT-INVALID, no spend.
rows.rationale: The internalisation instrument executed at $0 before any GPU: the claim can only attach where the model's own training bytes cannot answer by lookup.

rows.claim: The refusal surface is the named phrase 'cannot be determined from these facts' scored as a 24th forced-choice option appended to the 23-word CLUTRR menu, with a refusal_note line in every rules-2 prompt (train and eval); the 24-option decode is applied IDENTICALLY to every rules-2 arm (B0/B1/B2/B3/B5/c1p) on byte-identical prompts (rework-1, review fix 1); sum-of-token-logprob scoring length-penalises the longer refusal option UNIFORMLY across all compared arms — disclosed, not corrected. REWORK-2 disclosure: with the rules-1-b direction-explicit infill cue, the refusal option continues the cue agrammatically ('... is {a}'s cannot be determined from these facts'); the marked continuation is IDENTICAL across arms and across train/eval (family-3 training uses exactly this surface), so every between-arm refusal contrast is like-for-like; disclosed, not corrected.
rows.rationale: Trains and scores the fail-closed boundary on one commensurable surface; the known length bias cancels in every between-arm contrast.

rows.claim: B1 size matching (ASM-1425 'matched corpus size') is implemented as deterministic cyclic repetition of the sha-sorted stated+refusal train ids until |B1| == |B2| = 11,411 (rework-2 corpus; pinned in data/rules2-train/b1-upsample.json); token-count parity is approximate (repeated stated examples are shorter than entailed ones) and the actual per-arm train token counts are printed by --dry-plan and recorded in the training ledger.
rows.rationale: Example-count parity with full disclosure of the residual token-count delta beats an undisclosed mismatch or fabricated filler content.

rows.claim: c1' is a LABEL-derangement (seed 20260713) of family-2 TRAIN targets WITHIN each menu class, pinned in data/rules2-train/c1shuf-map.json (rework-1, review fix 2 — the former ITEM-Sattolo left 3,090/11,418 targets label-correct (pre-rework-2 corpus) because distinct items share answers): items are ordered label-block-contiguous (blocks by descending count, seeded shuffle within block) and the label sequence is rotated by the largest block size; shuffled_answer != original_answer is ASSERTED for EVERY item (fail-closed ERR_SHUFFLE; 0/11,411 label-correct in the rework-2 real build). The per-class label multiset (hence token count) is preserved EXACTLY for rel23 (max 2,572 of 10,121 <= n/2); for the imbalanced binary mw2 class (man 654 / woman 636) a multiset-preserving derangement is mathematically impossible by exactly 18 items, so those forced residuals are flipped to the complement label — a DISCLOSED multiset delta of 18 single-word targets (18/11,411 = 0.16% of family-2 train targets) recorded in c1shuf-map.json and the corpus manifest. Corpus size is identical to B2.
rows.backing_ref: PROPOSED-ASM-1426; H-STRUCT lineage (PROPOSED-ASM-1129); data/rules2-train/c1shuf-map.json disclosure; cross-vendor prereg review 2026-07-12 item 2
rows.rationale: Content destruction must be TOTAL at the per-item level for the collapse test to bite; the residual token-bulk delta is the smallest mathematically achievable and is disclosed, not silent.

rows.claim: LoRA hyperparameters are PINNED a priori in rules2-manifest.json (r=16, alpha=32, dropout=0, target q_proj/v_proj, lr=2e-4, 2 epochs, batch 16 via micro-batch 8, warmup 20, weight_decay 0, max_seq_len 512, AdamW, completion-only loss, fp32): NO hyperparameter search was performed anywhere in this build; the dev split feeds only a per-epoch loss ledger. Any pre-freeze HP change requires re-pinning the manifest and re-running --mock; any search must be dev-split-only and disclosed.
rows.rationale: Closes the train slot's cheapest lift-manufacturing path exactly as the design demands.

rows.claim: B4 is the A3 verify-retry loop executed VERBATIM through the imported, sha-pinned RULES-1-B rules1_runner.py bytes (sha 1f423ac0...; REWORK-2: rules-1's GPU run was VOIDED by the maintainer 2026-07-12 — a direction-ambiguous answer cue plus a cue-adjacent menu line kept every arm, including engine-direct A7, at ~0 — and superseded by the FROZEN rules-1-b record, whose direction-explicit frames fix both defects; importing the voided bytes would rebuild the broken instrument), frames from the rules-1 manifest (rules-1-b frames), k=4, fp32 R1, f2bt seeded resampler SEED_BASE 20260710, gold-leak guard, over the identical 858-item S-out slice, seeds {0,1,2}; its E5 control rows are engine-decided named refusals emitted under arm=B4. B4 is exempt from the rules-2 in-process repeat gate: its determinism instrument is the rules-1 decision-sha double-run discipline, unchanged.
rows.backing_ref: PROPOSED-ASM-1428; registry/experiments/rules-1-b.json (FROZEN, supersedes rules-1); docs/next/analysis/rules1-void-degenerate-instrument.md; poc/rules-2/rules2_runner.py B4 branch
rows.rationale: The gap-closure comparator must be the frozen comparator, not a reimplementation.

rows.claim: Eval decoding: 'greedy fp32' is implemented as the f2bt forced-choice scorer at attempt 0 (argmax over option logprobs — greedy on the closed surface, deterministic); G4 is an IN-PROCESS byte-identical repeat per arm (first seed) over the pinned first-200-by-sha S-out covered subsample, recorded as repeat_shas in the results bytes and read by the analysis repeat gate.
rows.rationale: Identical scorer bytes to every prior estate host run; the subsample repeat is the same determinism instrument at 23% of the token cost.

rows.claim: Prompt-format assignment (rework-1 review fix 1 + REWORK-2 rules-1-b adoption): EVERY rules-2 arm (B0/B1/B2/B3/B5/c1p) evaluates S-out and the corpus strata on BYTE-IDENTICAL prompts (the prompt is a pure function of the cell; no per-arm padding; shared prompt-surface sha recorded in the results bytes) with the SAME 24-option decode. The surface is the RULES-1-B frame contract — the closed menu rendered INSIDE the task header and a DIRECTION-EXPLICIT infill answer cue derived deterministically from the question's own closed template ('\nAnswer: {b} is {a}'s' for 'How is A related to B?'; '\nAnswer: {e} is a' for the E2 typing question; fail-closed ERR_CUE on any other shape; no gold dependence) — applied IDENTICALLY at train and eval time; the rules-1 void showed the former bare cue makes the gold word structurally un-elicitable, so retaining it would have rebuilt the voided instrument at the train slot. The s3' gap and its descriptive fraction are scored ONLY on a COMMON 23-option surface: B2 (all FT seeds) and B0 are additionally scored on the RULES-1-B A1-verbatim prompt (rules-1-b frames, 23-word menu, no refusal option — byte-identical to B4's attempt-0 verify-retry prompt), emitted as cell='entailed23' (R1); B2/B4 contrasts never cross surfaces. B3's proof remains a TRAINING-prompt block (scaffold), byte-equal to the rules-1-b derived block (asserted), so its eval surface stays commensurable with B2 (disclosed).
rows.backing_ref: cross-vendor prereg review 2026-07-12 item 1; rules-1-b frame fixes (PROPOSED-ASM-1630 lineage; docs/next/analysis/rules1-void-degenerate-instrument.md); rules2_runner.py render_prompt/render_cue/gap23_cells; results sout_prompt_surface_sha256/gap23_prompt_surface_sha256
rows.rationale: A primary contrast on non-identical prompt bytes confounds internalisation with surface effects; the gap-closure comparison must share one decode surface with the engine comparator.

rows.claim: Host revisions: R1 12fd25f7... and R3 31b70e2e... verbatim from the rules-1 manifest (f2b-replicate/f2b-transfer FROZEN chain); R2 = HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-360M-Instruct@a10cc1512eabd3dde888204e902eca88bddb4951 from the a5-llm pinned chain (docs/design-a5-llm.md).
rows.rationale: All three rungs resolve to already-pinned estate revisions; nothing newly floating.

rows.claim: Budget: usd_cap $18 / 14 GPU-h / 24 h wall for the DEFAULT R1 tier (rework-2 dry-plan on actual corpus chars, sharded: est $9.28, worst $13.92, 12.66 worst GPU-h on A10G summed over 17 independent jobs, worst single job 1.26 h vs the 12 h Modal function timeout); the R2 rung (B0/B1/B2, MD-R2-3) is a SECOND coordinator-authorized launch (7 jobs, rework-2 dry-plan worst $11.78; combined worst $25.70) inside the $35 outer ceiling, matching the design's $25-35 worst-case band; dry-plan fails closed on either cap AND on the per-job 12 h bound.
rows.backing_ref: PROPOSED-ASM-1434; rules2_runner.py --dry-plan output 2026-07-12 (rework-2, R1 + R2 tiers)
rows.rationale: The binding caps are registry numbers verified by a $0 plan against the real corpus, not the design's estimate bands.
rows.resolution_path: resolved by the run itself: the experiment-runner's per-tier --dry-plan gate fails closed before launch, and the post-run measured GPU-h/$ ledger (results.training_ledger + eval_ledger, /analysis/efficiency_ledger) replaces this extrapolation with measurements; if the measured R1-tier cost exceeds the $18 cap the run stops and this row is re-tagged with the measured band.

rows.claim: Mock mode is a SYNTHETIC stub (no torch/peft) whose draw streams are shared across arms of the same class so the paired analysis sees the planted contrast, not stub noise; the pins, c8 gate, corpus loading, prompt construction, B4 engine loop (real twin engine), row emitter, repeat gate and the full analysis contract are the REAL code paths; mock numbers are labelled MOCK end-to-end and are never measurements. REWORK-2 (review item 9): the green mock is PINNED as poc/rules-2/results/mock-validation.json (sha in the DRAFT record's artifact_hashes), produced by validate_mock.py: monolithic mock + the 13 canonical mock shard jobs + merge_shards.py, pinned-analysis parity between the two paths (identical except the disclosed process-measured metrics), verdict-rule mapping resolution over the mock analysis, and green R1+R2 dry-plans — all tied to the staged-bytes manifest sha; modal_rules2._launch_gates refuses any full run whose staged bytes do not match the pinned artifact's sha.
rows.rationale: Green mock = harness mechanics + analysis contract validated at $0, with no risk of a stub number being read as evidence.

rows.claim: E2 typing cells use the two-word menu {man, woman} (+ the refusal option) and appear in training and in the S-mem/S-held descriptives only; the primary and all secondaries are computed on rel23-surface cells; typing internalisation is reported under the regime stratification, never pooled into S-out.
rows.rationale: Keeps the primary on the single third-party-gold surface while retaining the design's E2 regime coverage.

rows.claim: Descriptive strata are evaluated on pinned sha-sorted subsamples (s_mem 200, s_held 200, stated_guard 150, refusal_guard 100 from data/rules2-train/eval-samples.json) and on the FIRST fine-tune seed only; the primary stratum S-out keeps all 858 items x all 3 FT seeds. The s4' guard therefore reads seed-0 stated/refusal cells (disclosed).
rows.rationale: Concentrates the GPU budget on the claim-carrying stratum; every cut is pinned and disclosed rather than silent.

rows.claim: Verdict mapping (kot-reg/2 verdict_rules) and statistics (rework-1, review fix 3): INSTRUMENT-INVALID unless (pin_gate AND c8_lookup_gate AND headroom AND repeat) all hold; FAIL on /analysis/kill_d_fired (KILL-d: B2-B0 S-out BCa LB95 <= 0); PASS on primary AND s1p AND s2p; else INCONCLUSIVE. The PRIMARY uses the registered SESOI 0.05 as a decision band: primary_pass requires LB95 > 0.05, KILL-d fires on LB95 <= 0, and 0 < LB95 <= 0.05 is the pre-registered INCONCLUSIVE band (neither pass nor kill). Every interval/p-value comes from a TWO-STAGE bootstrap (B=10000, seed 20260712) that resamples the FT-seed axis (cluster, independent per arm) before the item axis, so fine-tune-seed uncertainty enters the inference — never item-only. Secondaries are a REAL Holm step-down over the family {s1p, s2p, s3p, s4p}: one-sided bootstrap p-values (BCa inversion; percentile with conservative +inf replicates for the s1' ratio; intersection-union max-p for the two s4' components), ordered, compared to alpha/(m-rank) with the step-down stop rule; adjusted p-values and bounds at each test's assigned alpha are emitted in /analysis/holm; s3p is conditional on the RULES-1 A3 lift and DROPS OUT of the family (m adjusts) when unevaluable — null, never a fail. Analysis output fields fixed in analysis/rules_2.py OUTPUT_FIELDS.
rows.backing_ref: PROPOSED-ASM-1424..1430; registry/experiments/rules-2.json endpoints.primary.smallest_effect_of_interest; cross-vendor prereg review 2026-07-12 item 3; analysis/rules_2.py holm_stepdown/BootContrast
rows.rationale: The design's decision-rule sketch made mechanical with an ACTUAL multiplicity correction and seed-aware uncertainty, mirroring the frozen rules-1 rule shapes.

rows.claim: Freeze-time pin protocol: pins.harness_manifest is PINNED-AT-INPUTS (the staged-bytes sha printed by modal_rules2.py --print-manifest); materialiserPySha256 and analysisRules2PySha256 are PINNED-AT-FREEZE (the coordinator hashes the reviewed bytes, regenerates data/rules2-train + c8-result from them, and verifies the corpus digest and c8 sha match the record before prereg-freeze). Any post-freeze byte change requires a correction record before a final-phase run. REWORK-2 additions: (a) G1 ordering (review item 6) — the runner verifies the RAW BYTES of every imported pinned module (twin_engine.py, certificate.py, rules1_runner.py, f2bt_runner.py) and the certificate/c8 artifacts BEFORE any of them is imported; imports are lazy and fail closed; (b) the shard-merge protocol — merge_shards.py pools shards ONLY when pins, certificate precondition, c8 gate, prompt-surface shas, mode and canonical seed match byte-identically, ledger keys and row tuples are disjoint, and per-arm seed/strata/repeat coverage equals the design plan; (c) the PINNED-AT-INPUTS staged sha at rework-2 build time is 56c713d44b90ddc47d469b63ac2614c0ab72cf60be3db2f7c5f59b6baf8bc454.
rows.rationale: The generator-manifest-embeds-its-own-sha loop means the corpus digest is only final once the materialiser bytes are; the protocol makes that ordering explicit and fail-closed.
